prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response: Waking up is a severely overrated product with inconsistent performance and poor user support. The initial activation is jarringly abrupt, often triggered by an unreliable internal alarm system or external stimuli beyond user control. Battery life (i.e., sleep duration) rarely meets advertised specifications, and the interface—comprising grogginess, disorientation, and immediate existential dread—lacks intuitive design. While occasional firmware updates (e.g., weekends or vacations) marginally improve functionality, the core experience remains fundamentally flawed, offering minimal customization and zero refund options. Not recommended for anyone seeking a seamless start to their operational cycle.
